Tajika et al. concluded that sample F07 formed at a higher point in the water column than did M24.

In nautilids, sex predicts septa growth rate more strongly than does oxygen-18 concentration in the surrounding water.
Regardless of sex, nautilids spend most of their lives at depths of 50 meters or greater, where oxygen-18 concentrations typically differ from those higher in the water column.

questions.passage:

While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• The Mason Neck National Wildlife Refuge (NWR) is a protected natural area in Virginia.
• It encompasses 2,276 acres.
• It was established to safeguard the habitat of the bald eagle, an endangered species.
• The Mason Neck NWR is managed by the US Fish & Wildlife Service.
• The US Fish & Wildlife Service limits human activities in the area.
questions.questionText: The student wants to indicate the size of the Mason Neck NWR. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The Mason Neck NWR is a protected natural area managed by the US Fish & Wildlife Service, which limits human activities there.
A protected natural area, the Mason Neck NWR encompasses 2,276 acres of land in Virginia.
The Mason Neck NWR is a natural area in Virginia, home to the bald eagle.
Home to the bald eagle, Virginia's Mason Neck NWR is managed by the US Fish & Wildlife Service.

questions.passage:
The student wants to explain an advantage of the Spruce Tree House dwelling site. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Spruce Tree House is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site located in southwestern Colorado.
• It was built under a rock overhang and inhabited from approximately 1200-1280 CE.
• The overhanging rock ledges offered protection from heavy rain and snow.
• Ansel Hall is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site located in southwestern Colorado.
• It was built on relatively flat terrain and inhabited from approximately 1050-1160 CE.
• This level surface allowed for the construction of large terraced buildings.
questions.questionText: The student wants to explain an advantage of the Spruce Tree House dwelling site. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Since it was built under a rock overhang, Spruce Tree House was naturally protected from heavy rain and snow.
The location of Spruce Tree House, an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site in southwestern Colorado, provided an advantage to its inhabitants.
Located in southwestern Colorado, Spruce Tree House is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site that was inhabited from approximately 1200-1280 CE.
The relatively flat terrain on which Spruce Tree House was built allowed for the construction of large terraced buildings.

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form C SAT English Module 2
questions.prompt: The student wants to provide an explanation and example of Kepler's first law of planetary motion. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
questions.passage:
The student wants to provide an explanation and example of Kepler's first law of planetary motion. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Kepler's first law of planetary motion states that the orbit of a planet around the Sun is an ellipse.
• The law also states that, in an elliptical orbit, the object being orbited is one of the ellipse's foci.
• Kepler's laws of planetary motion also apply to natural satellites (e.g., moons).
• Tethys is a moon of Saturn that orbits the planet in 1.89 Earth days on average.
• Tethys's orbit is elliptical.
questions.questionText: The student wants to provide an explanation and example of Kepler's first law of planetary motion. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Saturn's moon Tethys completes an orbit in 1.89 Earth days on average, a clear example of Kepler's first law of planetary motion, which describes the elliptical orbit of planets.
Tethys's orbit of Saturn is elliptical, demonstrating Kepler's first law of planetary motion, which describes the elliptical orbits of planets but applies to moons as well.
Kepler's first law of planetary motion, which describes the orbits of Saturn and other planets in the solar system, states that the object being orbited is one of the ellipse's foci.
Kepler's first law of planetary motion states that the orbit of a planet around the Sun is an ellipse: for example, planetary satellites orbit their planets in an elliptical fashion.
